title: rubus × semivepallidus
intro: Rubus × semivepallidus, also known as the hybrid raspberry or semivepallid raspberry, is a hybrid plant species of the genus Rubus. It is a cross between Rubus idaeus and Rubus fruticosus, exhibiting characteristics of both parent species. The medicinal properties of Rubus × semivepallidus include its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, and it can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or juice for herbal medicinal use.
